text.title: go-http-handlers Core Rules
text.summary: Eight testable rules covering middleware order, explicit composition, context handling, handler thinness, and server timeouts.

rule.statement: Register middleware in this order: Recovery first, Logger second, RequestID third, then auth and domain-specific middleware.
rule.rationale: Recovery must catch panics from every later middleware; Logger needs RequestID earliest; auth depends on RequestID.

rule.statement: Use gin.New() instead of gin.Default(). Explicitly register each middleware.
rule.rationale: gin.Default() bundles Recovery + Logger with opinionated formats; explicit composition lets you align with your logger and trace conventions.

rule.statement: Use c.Request.Context() (or echo.Request().Context()) everywhere. Pass it to every service, repository, and database call.
rule.rationale: Request-scoped ctx carries cancellation, deadlines, and trace IDs through the call chain.

rule.statement: Never create context.Background() inside a handler. context.Background() is for main() and test setup only.
rule.rationale: Using Background() in a handler breaks cancellation propagation — long-running downstream calls won't stop when the client disconnects.

rule.statement: Do not spawn goroutines inside a handler that use c.Request.Context(). The context is cancelled when the handler returns; goroutines then crash on use.
rule.rationale: Request ctx is bound to the handler lifetime; for background work, derive a new ctx (context.WithoutCancel + WithTimeout) and pass extracted values.

rule.statement: Keep handlers to: bind request -> validate -> call one service method -> translate to HTTP. No business logic in handler files.
rule.rationale: Thin handlers are testable as transport adapters; logic in handlers becomes untestable without a real HTTP stack.

rule.statement: Validate in the request struct via binding tags (`binding:"required,email,gte=0"`), not in the handler body.
rule.rationale: Centralizing validation in the DTO definition makes contracts inspectable and removes nested ifs from handlers.

rule.statement: Always set ReadHeaderTimeout, ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, and IdleTimeout on http.Server. Never leave default zero values.
rule.rationale: Zero timeouts means a slow client can hold a connection forever (Slowloris); ReadHeaderTimeout=5s, ReadTimeout=30s, WriteTimeout=30s, IdleTimeout=120s are reasonable defaults.

rule.statement: gin.New(), explicit order, ctx plumbed, thin handlers, binding-tag validation, timeouts set, graceful shutdown wired.
rule.rationale: Aggregate conclusion target for the decision tree's positive branch.

rule.statement: Service is not HTTP or does not own its lifecycle (serverless) — methodology does not apply.
rule.rationale: Aggregate conclusion target for the decision tree's negative branch.
